conjecture_title: Informal Conjecture: r(K^(3)_{4-e}, S^(3)_n) = Theta(n^2 / log n)
paper_title: Hypergraph Ramsey numbers of cliques versus stars
summary: The conjecture that $r(K^{(3)}_{4-e}, S^{(3)}_n) = \Theta(n^2/\log n)$ remains open. The source paper (Proposition 1.1) establishes the bounds $c\,n^2/\log^2 n \le r(K^{(3)}_{4-e}, S^{(3)}_n) \le c'\,n^2/\log n$, leaving a logarithmic gap. A targeted search of the 2022–2026 literature found no follow-up paper closing this gap; the two most relevant subsequent papers on off-diagonal hypergraph Ramsey numbers (arXiv:2404.02021 and arXiv:2411.13812) address clique-vs-clique settings rather than clique-vs-star.
search_queries: hypergraph Ramsey K4-e star 3-uniform r(K4-e, S_n) Theta n^2/log n
arXiv 2210.03545 Conlon Fox He Mubayi Suk Verstraete hypergraph Ramsey cliques stars follow-up
notes: No follow-up resolving the conjecture found after 5 web calls. The closest related papers (arXiv:2404.02021, arXiv:2411.13812) concern off-diagonal Ramsey numbers against complete hypergraphs K_n^(3), not against stars S_n^(3). The conjecture is closely related to the classical result r(K_3, K_n) = Theta(n^2/log n) via the analogy noted by the authors; the suggested proof strategy via the (K^{(3)}_{4-e})-free process has not been carried out in the indexed literature.
